briefdescription.para: Key event signal type.
Event Processing finished signal type.
Touch signal type.
Wheel signal type.
Context status signal type.
Scene created signal type.
Allows the creation of an empty stage handle.

detaileddescription.para: To retrieve the current stage, this handle can be set using Stage::GetCurrent().

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the copied handle

briefdescription.para: This move constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

briefdescription.para: This move assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

parameterdescription.para: A reference to the moved handle

briefdescription.para: Adds a child Actor to the Stage.

detaileddescription.para:
The child will be referenced.
actor
The child
The actor has been initialized.
The actor does not have a parent.

briefdescription.para: Removes a child Actor from the Stage.

detaileddescription.para:
The child will be unreferenced.
actor
The child
The actor has been added to the stage.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of the Stage in pixels as a Vector.

detaileddescription.para:
The x component will be the width of the Stage in pixels. The y component will be the height of the Stage in pixels. The z component will be the distance between far and near planes.
The size of the Stage as a Vector

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the list of render-tasks.

simplesect.para: A valid handle to a RenderTaskList

briefdescription.para: Queries the number of on-stage layers.

detaileddescription.para:
Note that a default layer is always provided (count >= 1).
The number of layers

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the layer at a specified depth.

simplesect.para: The layer found at the given depth
Depth is less than layer count; see GetLayerCount().

briefdescription.para: Returns the Stage's Root Layer.
Sets the background color of the stage.
Retrieves the background color of the stage.
Retrieves the DPI of the display device to which the stage is connected.
Gets the Object registry.
Keep rendering for at least the given amount of time.

detaileddescription.para:
By default, Dali will stop rendering when no Actor positions are being set, and when no animations are running etc. This method is useful to force screen refreshes e.g. when updating a NativeImage.
durationSeconds
Time to keep rendering, 0 means render at least one more frame

briefdescription.para: Requests forced rendering for this scene.

parameterdescription.para: The number of frames to render forcibly. @SINCE_2_5.17

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when key event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted just after the event processing is finished.
This signal is emitted when the screen is touched and when the touch ends (i.e. the down & up touch events only).

detaileddescription.para: If there are multiple touch points, then this will be emitted when the first touch occurs and then when the last finger is lifted. An interrupted event will also be emitted (if it occurs). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(TouchEventevent);

simplesect.para: The touch signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.

detaileddescription.para:
A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the GL context is lost (Platform specific behaviour).

detaileddescription.para:
If the application is responsible for handling context loss, it should listen to this signal and tear down UI components when received.
The context lost signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the GL context is regained (Platform specific behavior).

detaileddescription.para:
If the application is responsible for handling context loss, it should listen to this signal and rebuild UI components on receipt.
The context regained signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted after the initial scene is created.

detaileddescription.para: It will be triggered after the application init signal.

A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName();
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: Gets the current Stage.

simplesect.para: The current stage or an empty handle if the internal core has not been created or has been already destroyed

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the Stage exists; this should only return false during or after destruction of Dali core.

simplesect.para: True when it's safe to call Stage::GetCurrent()

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the Stage shutting down now; this should only return false during or before destruction of Dali core.

simplesect.para: True when Dali core destructor called.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether we installed Dali core before, or not. It will be useful whether you want to check we are on valid core ui thread or not, after Core initalized ensured.

simplesect.para: True when this API called at core ui thread. False otherwise.

inbodydescription.para: < Check if Core is installed now,
< or Core is shutting down now.

briefdescription.para: The Stage is a top-level object used for displaying a tree of Actors.

detaileddescription.para: Stage is a top-level object that represents the entire screen. It is used for displaying a hierarchy of actors managed by the scene graph structure, which means an actor inherits a position relative to its parent, and can be moved in relation to this point.
The stage instance is a singleton object (the only instance of its class during the lifetime of the program). You can get it using a static function.
To display the contents of an actor, it must be added to a stage. The following example shows how to connect a new actor to the stage: Actoractor=Actor::New(); Stage::GetCurrent().Add(actor);
The stage has a 2D size that matches the size of the application window. The default unit 1 is 1 pixel with the default camera.
Multiple stage/window support is not currently provided.

Signals
Signal Name
Method
keyEvent
KeyEventSignal()
eventProcessingFinished
EventProcessingFinishedSignal()
touched
TouchedSignal()
wheelEvent
WheelEventSignal()
contextLost
ContextLostSignal()
contextRegained
ContextRegainedSignal()
sceneCreated
SceneCreatedSignal()
